memory: Owner-scoped queue-update files are not themselves reducer records. They require an explicit, fail-closed normalization boundary that maps only recognized operational fields into JSON-pointer patches, rejects owner and source-path mismatches, and records omitted fields rather than silently interpreting them.

observed: The effective-state reducer requires explicit patches and repository provenance.
Existing queue-update records use a higher-level operational shape.
The committed normalizer passed five deterministic Node tests before commit.
inferred: A narrow allowlist is safer than generic recursive conversion because queue updates also contain evidence, privacy, and rollback metadata that should not automatically become effective queue state.
proposed: Add repository-ancestry derivation and batch normalization over all queue-update classes before materializing effective state.
unresolved: The complete set of historical queue-update schemas has not yet been enumerated.
Commit reachability is still caller-supplied to the reducer.

privacy_boundary: Public repository governance, queue identifiers, abstract reconciliation semantics, paths, and commit IDs only. No private chat excerpts, health or pet-health material, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploads were retained.
